claim: Every message in a Claude Code session reintroduces all system context; message N costs N times more tokens than message 1
actionable: Start fresh sessions between unrelated tasks to avoid compounding context costs across long conversations
source_title: I Stopped Hitting Claude Code Usage Limits

claim: MCP servers load all tool definitions into context on every message, not just when called—disconnect unused MCPs per session
actionable: Run /mcp at session start and disconnect MCPs you're not using to save thousands of tokens per message
source_title: I Stopped Hitting Claude Code Usage Limits

claim: Replacing MCP servers with CLI equivalents saves 40% token usage because CLIs only cost tokens when executed
actionable: Use CLI tools instead of MCPs when available to reduce baseline context bloat
source_title: I Stopped Hitting Claude Code Usage Limits

claim: Bloated Claude.md files reduce output quality because LLMs focus on tail context; progressive disclosure (references to separate files) improves results
actionable: Move detailed rules to separate reference files and link them from Claude.md with one-liners; let agents fetch context on demand
source_title: I Stopped Hitting Claude Code Usage Limits

claim: Setting auto_compact_percentage_override to 75% triggers compaction earlier than default 83%, preserving output quality
actionable: Add auto_compact_percentage_override: 0.75 to Claude Code settings.json to improve output before reaching hard context limits
source_title: I Stopped Hitting Claude Code Usage Limits

claim: Bash output truncation wastes tokens—set bash_max_output_length to 150K to reduce retry loops from silent truncation
actionable: Configure bash_max_output_length environment variable to 150000 to avoid silent command truncation that forces reruns
source_title: I Stopped Hitting Claude Code Usage Limits

claim: Using plan mode before complex tasks prevents costly wrong-path implementations by forcing early alignment
actionable: Always run plan mode for non-trivial tasks to catch misunderstandings before agents write unnecessary code
source_title: I Stopped Hitting Claude Code Usage Limits

claim: Replacing agent corrections with fresh sessions eliminates compounded context pollution from failed attempts
actionable: When an agent produces bad output, use /clear and restart rather than following up with corrections in the same session
source_title: I Stopped Hitting Claude Code Usage Limits
